title: Russia-North Korea Cooperation Sanctions Act
plain_summary: This bill creates new punishments for people and groups that help North Korea give weapons to Russia for the war in Ukraine. The President must block the money and property of foreign people, companies, and banks that help move weapons between North Korea and Russia. These people and groups also cannot enter the United States. The bill also requires the government to regularly tell Congress about how North Korea is helping Russia in the war.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, Ways and Means, Financial Services, and Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequent

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Russia-North Korea Cooperation Sanctions Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts. On February twenty fourth, two thousand twenty two, Russia's government, led by Vladimir Putin, started an unprovoked, full-scale attack on Ukraine. On September thirteenth, two thousand twenty three, North Korea's leader Kim Jong Un met with Russian President Vladimir Putin to discuss giving or selling weapons to Russia for use in Ukraine's invasion. In exchange, Russia would provide economic help and support for North Korea's space, nuclear, and missile programs. This was Kim Jong Un's first known trip outside North Korea since the COVID-nineteen pandemic started and North Korea locked down in early two thousand twenty. North Korea has a large stockpile of artillery shells, rockets, and other military equipment that it could give to Russia. If Russia or Russian government-backed groups are getting weapons and related materials from North Korea, these actions would break United Nations Security Council rules that Russia supported. These rules ban North Korea from selling such items to other countries. On August sixteenth, two thousand twenty three, the US Treasury Department put sanctions on three groups connected to a network trying to help arms deals between Russia and North Korea.

sections.title: Section 3 Sanctions
sections.text: The President must impose the punishments described below on the following people and organizations. First, any foreign person who is responsible for or helps move weapons or material support from North Korea to be used in Russia's illegal war in Ukraine. Second, any foreign person who knowingly brings in, sends out, or re-exports to or from North Korea any goods, services, or technology for weapons that Russian forces or their allies might use in Russia's illegal war in Ukraine. Third, any foreign financial institution that knowingly helps with a major transaction or provides major financial services for the people described above. Fourth, any foreign person who engages in a major transaction related to moving or selling weapons or material support with the people or institutions described above. Fifth, any foreign person helping with the shipping and movement of North Korean weapons or material support to be used in Russia's illegal war in Ukraine. The punishments are as follows. First, blocking property. Even though certain requirements normally apply under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the President can use all powers granted by that Act to block and ban all transactions involving all property and property interests of the foreign person if such property is in the United States, comes into the United States, or is controlled by a US person. Second, visas, admission, or parole. Any foreign person that the Secretary of State or Secretary of Homeland Security knows or has reason to believe falls under the categories above cannot enter the United States, cannot get a visa or other documents to enter the United States, and cannot be admitted or paroled into the United States or receive any other benefit under immigration law. Any visa or entry document already given to such a person must be cancelled immediately by the issuing officer or Secretary. This cancellation takes effect right away and automatically cancels any other valid visa or entry document the person has. The President can use all authorities provided under sections two hundred three and two hundred five of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to carry out this section. The same penalties that apply under section two hundred six of that Act will apply to anyone who violates, tries to violate, conspires to violate, or causes a violation of rules made to carry out this section. The President can waive these sanctions for a foreign person or foreign financial institution if the President tells the appropriate congressional committees at least fifteen days before the waiver takes effect that the waiver is vital to US national security interests. Internationally recognized humanitarian organizations will not be subject to these sanctions if they engage in financial transactions for humanitarian assistance under a waiver, transport goods or services necessary for humanitarian operations under a waiver, or have only incidental contact with sanctioned individuals while providing humanitarian assistance under a waiver.

sections.title: Section 4 Expansion of North Korea Sanctions and Policy Enhancement Act of 2016
sections.text: The North Korea Sanctions and Policy Enhancement Act of two thousand sixteen is changed as follows. In section four hundred one, a new goal is added: halting material support for Russia's illegal war in Ukraine. Material support has the meaning given in section six of this Russia-North Korea Cooperation Sanctions Act. In section four hundred two, a new element is added to the policy: halting material support for Russia's illegal war in Ukraine, where material support has the meaning given in section six of this Act.

sections.title: Section 5 Report
sections.text: No later than ninety days after this Act becomes law, and every one hundred eighty days after that, the President must give the appropriate congressional committees a report that describes major activities by North Korea to support Russia and its allies in Russia's illegal war in Ukraine. The report must include the following information. First, the identity and nationality of foreign people and foreign financial institutions that are subject to sanctions under section three. Second, a description of what these people and institutions did. Third, an assessment of how much a foreign government has provided material support to North Korea's government or anyone acting for that government to conduct major activities that materially support Russia's illegal war in Ukraine. Fourth, a US strategy to counter North Korea's efforts to conduct major activities supporting Russia's war in Ukraine, including efforts to work with foreign governments to stop North Korea's government and people acting for that government from conducting major activities supporting Russia's illegal war in Ukraine. The report must be submitted in unclassified form, but can include a classified section.

sections.title: Section 6 Definitions
sections.text: In this Act, the following terms have these meanings. Appropriate congressional committees means the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate. Foreign financial institution has the meaning given in section one thousand ten point six hundred five of title thirty one, Code of Federal Regulations, and includes a foreign central bank. Material support has the same meaning as material support or resources in section two thousand three hundred thirty nine A of title eighteen, United States Code.
